content:
Italian Alpine peak topped with jagged cable car station

Milan-based Studio di Architettura De Carlo Gualla has created the Stella di Pila cable car station with panoramic views of the Italian Alps. Surrounded by mountains at a height of 2,723 metres, the cable car station, which tops the Aosta-Pila-Couis Gondola, was designed to be a landmark within the Alpine landscape of the Aosta Valley region.

The post Italian Alpine peak topped with jagged cable car station appeared first on Dezeen.
